Closest Hospital to the Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek
retrace your way back to Lohajung (base camp)
return to Wan, which has the closest motorable roadhead

value: Retrace way back to Lohajung (base camp)
Descend to Didna village then trek back to Lohajung

evidence.quote_or_summary: In case of an emergency, medical or otherwise, the best way is to retrace your way back to Lohajung (base camp). You have to return to Wan, which has the closest motorable roadhead. Descending to Didna village to rest and recuperate there is also an option. But you will have to trek back to Lohajung from Didna.

value: Primary Health Care Center in Dewal, about 30 km from the Indiahikes Lohajung Campus (very basic facilities)
Hospitals in Gopeshwar (Chamoli District HQ), about 120 km by road (4-hour drive) from Lohajung
Hospitals at Rudraprayag (faster to access, downhill journey from Lohajung)

evidence.quote_or_summary: There is a Primary Health Care Center in Dewal, about 30 km from the Indiahikes Lohajung Campus. However, it has very basic facilities. For more serious concerns, you need to approach the hospitals in Gopeshwar, the Headquarters of Chamoli District. It is about 120 km by road (4-hour drive) from Lohajung. Accessing the hospitals at Rudraprayag is faster since it is a downhill journey from Lohajung while Gopeshwar is an uphill drive.
Easy-Moderate
Suitable for Fit Beginners
You will cover a total of around 27.2 km in four trekking days

value: Every day includes steep ascents and descents
Day 1: gain nearly 3000 ft over 6 km
Day 3: steep ascent before crossing treeline at onset of Bedni Bugyal (Gehroli Patal to Abin Kharak)
Several steep sections require alertness and careful trekking

evidence.quote_or_summary: Every day will include steep ascents and descents. Several steep sections on this trek require you to be alert and trek carefully. On the first day of your trek, you gain nearly 3000 ft in elevation over a 6 km stretch. On Day 3, you will trek from Gehroli Patal to Abin Kharak. This involves a steep ascent before crossing the treeline at the onset of the Bedni Bugyal.

value: Snowfall distinct possibility near Bedni Top, especially in winter months, obstructing paths
Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites or turn them into slippery slush
Neelganga River crossing risky in heavy rain

evidence.quote_or_summary: On this trek, snowfall is a distinct possibility near Bedni Top, especially in the winter months. Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths. Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites or turn them into slippery slush. It can also make the Neelganga River crossing risky.

value: Ascend almost 3000 ft from Lohajung to Wan Kharak in one day
Bedni Top at 12,550 ft; above 10,000 ft every trekker susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness

evidence.quote_or_summary: From Lohajung to Wan Kharak, you ascend almost 3000 ft in one day. Bedni Top is situated 12,550 ft above sea level and at altitudes above 10,000 ft, every trekker is susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness.
our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required
Suitable for Fit Beginners

value: Day 1 steep climb of nearly 3000 ft over 6 km will test fitness
Fitness helps make trek easier but does not eliminate AMS risk

evidence.quote_or_summary: This is a steep climb that will test your fitness. Fitness helps in making your trek easier. But do not forget that even a fit person climbing too high too soon runs an equal risk of developing AMS.
Suitable for Fit Beginners

evidence.quote_or_summary: snowfall is a distinct possibility near Bedni Top, especially in the winter months

extracted_items.value: Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites, turn them to slippery slush, and make Neelganga River crossing risky

evidence.quote_or_summary: Hours of rainfall or snowfall can completely alter the trek’s dynamics. Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites or turn them into slippery slush. It can also make the Neelganga River crossing risky.

value: Several steep sections require alertness and careful trekking

evidence.quote_or_summary: Several steep sections on this trek require you to be alert and trek carefully.

value: Snowfall obstructs previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks
Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites or turn them into slippery slush
Neelganga River crossing risky in heavy rain

evidence.quote_or_summary: Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut on the snow. Heavy rain may flood trails and campsites or turn them into slippery slush. It can also make the Neelganga River crossing risky.

value: Even fit person climbing too high too soon runs equal risk of developing AMS

evidence.quote_or_summary: at altitudes above 10,000 ft, every trekker is susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness. even a fit person climbing too high too soon runs an equal risk of developing AMS.

value: Technical team monitors weather; may turn back from any campsite if weather deteriorates
Indiahikes equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, stretchers
Inform Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions

evidence.quote_or_summary: Our technical team, in conjunction with the Trek Leader, constantly monitors the weather conditions and makes informed decisions about proceeding with or aborting the trek. If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite. The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers. Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns.

around 27.2 km in four trekking days

extracted_items.value: Safety is a shared responsibility; trekkers must actively participate and follow safety protocols

evidence.quote_or_summary: The Indiahikes team will ensure your safety throughout the journey. However, we believe that safety is a shared responsibility. We need your active participation and attention to safety protocols to ensure a safe and successful trek.
Section title and repeated references to 'Ali Bedni Bugyal trek' throughout the text
Bright red rhododendrons bloom along the trail, especially on the way to Gehroli Patal; clear, sharp views of Mt. Trishul and Mt. Nanda Ghunti
What makes it more special on the Ali Bedni trek is that the slope faces the west.

conditions: Last patches of snow melting away, with bits lingering on higher slopes
Forests waking up after winter with fresh green leaves appearing
Bright red rhododendrons bloom along the trail, especially on the way to Gehroli Patal
Forest alive with thrushes and barbets calling through the trees
Crisp and clean air with clear, sharp views of Mt. Trishul and Mt. Nanda Ghunti

evidence.quote_or_summary: Ali Bedni Bugyal in Spring (March-April) section describing snow melt, forest awakening, rhododendrons, bird calls, and mountain views

conditions: Best of this seen in June

evidence.quote_or_summary: The best time to do the Ali Bedni Bugyal trek would be in summer, for lush greenery in the meadows and wildflowers growing in abundance. You see the best of this in June.

conditions: Skies squeaky clean after monsoon clouds are washed away
Recommended for photographs of big mountains in the region

evidence.quote_or_summary: Another good time to do the Ali Bedni Bugyal trek is in the post-monsoon season... skies are squeaky clean and you get the best views of mountains... If you're one for photographs of big mountains in the region, choose the post-monsoon season.

conditions: Most beautiful snow cover among winter treks
Snow settles softly under the boughs of oaks and rhododendrons
West-facing slope receives early morning sun slanting through trees hitting snow at magical angles

evidence.quote_or_summary: Ali Bedni winter trek is the one with the most beautiful snow cover... snow settles softly under the boughs of the oaks and rhododendrons... slope faces the west... sun slants through the trees hitting the snow at magical angles.
Bright red rhododendrons bloom... wildflowers growing in abundance... boughs of the oaks and rhododendrons... snow covering the pine and oak forest
You'll also hear the forest coming alive with thrushes and barbets calling through the trees.
lush greenery in the meadows... snow covering the pine and oak forest... snow-covered summits gleaming in the sun
You get clear, sharp views of Mt. Trishul and Mt. Nanda Ghunti... their snow-covered summits gleaming in the sun.

extracted_items.value: Summer (May-June) for lush greenery in meadows and abundant wildflowers

evidence.quote_or_summary: The best time to do the Ali Bedni Bugyal trek would be in summer, for lush greenery in the meadows and wildflowers growing in abundance.

extracted_items.value: Post-monsoon (September-November) for best mountain views and photographs of big mountains

evidence.quote_or_summary: If you're one for photographs of big mountains in the region, choose the post-monsoon season.

extracted_items.value: Most beautiful snow cover compared to other winter treks offered

evidence.quote_or_summary: We have 7 winter treks — and all of them are great. But the Ali Bedni winter trek is the one with the most beautiful snow cover.

extracted_items.value: Able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 40 minutes

evidence.quote_or_summary: To do the Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek comfortably, you must be able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 40 minutes. This is the minimum recommended fitness level that you must aim for.
To reach any fitness target, there are two things you must work on: Cardiovascular Endurance, Muscle Strength
You need to train your core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings.
Walking, jogging or running is the best routine for this.
Follow this, and you'll be trek-ready in 6 weeks.

value.tracking_method: Track runs on Strava, Nike Run Club, or any similar fitness app
value.submission_requirement: Upload 3 screenshots of runs to Indiahikes Dashboard
value.consistency_criteria: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

evidence.quote_or_summary: Track your runs on Strava, Nike Run Club, or any similar fitness app. Take 3 screenshots of your runs and upload them to your Indiahikes Dashboard. Make sure you upload these screenshots 15 to 20 days before your trek date. We want to see that you are being consistent in your preparation. Your screenshots must demonstrate that you are meeting the fitness criteria for your trek.

value: Runs not meeting trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

evidence.quote_or_summary: Your fitness records will be rejected for one of two reasons: You haven't uploaded your 3 screenshots to your dashboard. Your runs don't meet your trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month.

value.leader_notification: Trek Leader and Slope Manager will be informed about fitness levels in advance
value.removal_protocol: If trekker falls behind the group by 45 minutes and is visibly struggling, Trek Leader may send them back

evidence.quote_or_summary: You can still trek. But your Trek Leader and Slope Manager will be informed about your fitness levels in advance. If you fall behind the group by 45 minutes and are visibly struggling, your Trek Leader may send you back.

extracted_items.value: Not required to submit fitness records

evidence.quote_or_summary: Children below 14 years are not required to submit fitness records.

extracted_items.value: Parents must ensure that children remain physically active and prepared for the trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: However, parents must ensure that children remain physically active and prepared for the trek.

extracted_items.value: Trekker may struggle, fall behind, and cause the entire team to slow down; other trekkers may miss summit or viewpoints

evidence.quote_or_summary: If you're not fit, you won't enjoy your trek. You'll struggle. You'll fall behind. And you'll miss out on the transformative experience we've designed for you. when you lag behind, you pull your entire team back. Your Trek Leader has to slow everyone down. The trekkers who trained hard and showed up prepared won't take this lightly. Nobody wants to miss a summit or a breathtaking viewpoint because someone didn't train.

extracted_items.value: If a trekker falls behind the group by 45 minutes and is visibly struggling, the Trek Leader may send them back

evidence.quote_or_summary: If you fall behind the group by 45 minutes and are visibly struggling, your Trek Leader may send you back. That's a call none of us want to make. But it is essential for your and the entire team’s safety.
you must digitally upload and carry mandatory documents to get government permits for the trek
the forest department will not issue permissions for your trek

Original and photocopy of government photo identity card: Anything such as a driver’s license, Aadhar Card, or passport will do. Note This is required by the forest department for your identification.

Medical certificate: The medical certificate needs to be filled out by an MBBS doctor, without which the forest department will not issue permissions for your trek. It is also a requirement by Indiahikes

If you don’t have certain items, you can rent high-quality gear like trekking shoes, backpacks, and jackets from the Crosstrek Rental Store by Indiahikes. It costs less than Rs 2000 for the entire trek.

extracted_items.value: less than Rs 2000 for the entire trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: It costs less than Rs 2000 for the entire trek.
At the end of the trek please donate unused medicines to your trek leader. Some of these medicines get distributed to villages on the trek
carrying your own personal backpack reduces your impact on the mountains by 20%
There are no printing or photocopy facilities at the basecamp.
Please note there is no facility to get printouts and photocopies at Sari.
Treadmill Test: If your age is 58 and above, you’ll need to submit a Treadmill Test (not older than 12 months) within a week of completing your trek registration.

extracted_items.value: disclaimer certificate signed by parents/guardian, printed and carried to basecamp

evidence.quote_or_summary: if you are a minor unaccompanied by your parents, please download this disclaimer, print it, get it signed by your parents/guardian and carry it to the basecamp.

Medical certificate: The medical certificate needs to be filled out by an MBBS doctor, without which the forest department will not issue permissions for your trek. It is also a requirement by Indiahikes
The medical certificate needs to be filled out by an MBBS doctor

Indiahikes has a panel of doctors who can help you with a medical certificate. They are available for online consultation. To get in touch with them, please write to trekmedicalcertificates@gmail.com. They will respond to you within 24 hours. Consultation fee: Rs 350
3 layers if you're trekking in spring, summer and monsoon; 4 layers if you're trekking in autumn; 5 layers if you're trekking in winter

3 layers if you're trekking in spring, summer and monsoon (1 woollen sweater, 1 fleece, 1 padded jacket); 4 layers if you're trekking in autumn (1 woollen sweater, 2 fleece, 1 padded jacket); 5 layers if you're trekking in winter (1 pair of thermals, 1 woollen sweater, 2 fleece, 1 padded jacket)
We expect all our trekkers to carry their own backpacks unless they have a justifiable reason not to.

extracted_items.value: carrying your own personal backpack reduces your impact on the mountains by 20%

evidence.quote_or_summary: Carrying your own personal backpack reduces your impact on the mountains by 20%.
Diamox (1 Strip): It is important to carry Diamox as part of the Live Saving Drugs. Read more about Diamox here on how it reduces the chances of Acute Mountain Sickness on the trek.
Digene (4 tablets): Take it if you feel the food that you’ve taken is undigested. Alert your trek leader immediately. It could be a sign of AMS.
Our trek leaders carry a high altitude medical kit with them which also consist of Life Saving Drugs.
our trek leaders carry medical kits with 17 medicines including life-saving drugs
Our trek leaders carry a high altitude medical kit with them which also consist of Life Saving Drugs.
you must bring your personal medical kit as well. We are already equipped with BP machines, oximeters and canned oxygen, but your medical kit is for quick access to you, especially while travelling.

value.purpose: reduces chances of Acute Mountain Sickness
pain reliever for muscle strain, contains paracetamol
undigested food, possible sign of AMS
replenish essential salts lost while trekking

evidence.quote_or_summary: Carry these medicines with you, easily accessible at all times. Do not take any medicine unless you have consulted your trek leader. [list follows]
Wet wipes are strictly not allowed on our treks

extracted_items.value: keep mandatory documents in plastic bag/cover to prevent getting wet

evidence.quote_or_summary: Keep them in a plastic bag to keep them safe in your backpack.
Keep important documents in a clear plastic cover and slide them into the inner pocket at the back of your backpack. This keeps them from getting wet.
Indiahikes practices low impact sustainable trekking in every aspect of the trek.
At the end of the trek please donate unused medicines to your trek leader. Some of these medicines get distributed to villages on the trek and some are added to the Indiahikes medical kit.

value: Cemented trail through Wan village houses
Centuries-old Cypress trees (15 minutes from Wan)
Gehroli Patal clearing with green trekker's hut

evidence.quote_or_summary: The initial trail from Wan is cemented through a cluster of houses. 15 minutes into your trek, you will find a cluster of centuries-old Cypress trees.
The second one acts as a deviation from the Latu Devta temple.
It takes 30-45 minutes to ascend from Wan to Ranaka Dhar.
From here, it is a short descent to the iconinc Neel Ganga. The bridge on Neel Ganga is an idyllic location to take a breather.
The trail now winds through Oak and Rhododendron forests
This is a trek of about 3 hours that brings you to a clearing with a green trekker’s hut
Set up camp here.
Difficulty: Easy-Moderate.

cluster of centuries-old Cypress trees
The trail now winds through Oak and Rhododendron forests
There are walnut, pear, Himalayan roses and other flora you can find on this climb.
You may even spot birds like a flycatcher, and magpies.

value: Latu Devta temple (local deity believed to protect the area)
Mountain folk pay obeisance to Latu Devta before ascending higher on the Nanda Devi Jat Yatra
Ringing the temple bell for a safe trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: Latu Devta is a local deity, who is believed to protect the area. The mountain folk pay obeisance to him before ascending higher on the Nanda Devi Jat Yatra. Ring the temple bell here for a safe trek

value: Views of Lohajung, Wan and the valley below from Ranaka Dhar
Neel Ganga river with overhanging trees and cool, refreshing water
Mt Trishul views from Gehroli Patal on clear days

evidence.quote_or_summary: At Ranaka Dhar, take in the view of Lohajung, Wan and the valley below!
Trees overhang the river and the water trips and fall over boulders in the shade. The water here is cool and refreshing.
On a clear day, you get astounding views of Mt Trishul commandeering over the area.

Start your day with a climb through the oak and Rhododendron forest... Short climb with rolling meadows... leading you straight to Bedni Bugyal... Takes a sharp turn on the ledge hugging the mountains

extracted_items.value: Climb through oak and rhododendron forest, steep ascent crossing tree line to Bedni Bugyal, 800 metre ascent to Bedni Top, descend to main trail (Roopkund trail), sharp turn on ledge hugging mountains, gradual descent to Ali Bugyal 2 km away, reach Abin Kharak in a depression on left side of ridge

evidence.quote_or_summary: Start your day with a climb through the oak and Rhododendron forest. The trail takes a steep ascent and crosses the tree line, leading you straight to Bedni Bugyal... just 800 metres away is the highest point on the trek, the Bedni Top... From Bedni Top, descend down to the main trail. You need to stick to this main trail. This is the trail which takes one to the famous trek of Roopkund... Takes a sharp turn on the ledge hugging the mountains... Now leisurely walk your way to Ali Bugyal, 2 km away... From Ali Bugyal, Abin Kharak campsite is on the left side of the ridge and in a depression in the bugyal.
Difficulty: Easy-Moderate.

value: The steep ascent to Bali Pass requires help from an expert guide.
While there are no crevasses, heavy snow makes an ice axe useful.
Use a scissor-bend approach instead of a straight-line climb.

The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders.
The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass.
The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous.

Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.

evidence.quote_or_summary: Terrain: The steep ascent to Bali Pass requires help from an expert guide. While there are no crevasses, heavy snow makes an ice axe useful. Use a scissor-bend approach instead of a straight-line climb. The famous knife-edge ridge leading to Bali Pass is a tricky traversal. With deep valleys on either side of the ridge, the walk can be nerve wracking. The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders. The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass. The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous. The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings. Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.
our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.

The climb to Advance Base Camp is extremely steep and involves trekking on huge boulders. The descent is extremely steep beyond the pass. The entire day from Advance Base Camp to Lower Damini across the Bali Pass is long and arduous. The trail runs along rivers, requiring frequent crossings. Bridges may be washed away, needing a rope and pulley system.

value: Rain makes trails slushy and difficult to navigate safely
Weather deterioration may require turning back from any campsite

evidence.quote_or_summary: Too much snow can make the pass inaccessible.
Rain can turn the trails slushy and difficult to navigate safely.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.

value: Evacuation may take up to 24 hours
Roads connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi are notoriously bad
Nearest hospital for major emergencies is in Mussoorie or Dehradun, 6-7 hours away
Basic medical centers can only treat very basic injuries like simple fractures, sprains etc.

evidence.quote_or_summary: Evacuation may take up to 24 hours since you are deep in the valley and the roads connecting Kotgaon, Purola, Janakichatti and Rajgarhi are notoriously bad.
for major medical emergencies, the nearest hospital is in Mussoorie or Dehradun – which is almost 6-7 hours away from both Kotgaon and Janakichatti.
these medical centers can only treat very basic injuries like simple fractures, sprains etc.

evidence.quote_or_summary: our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.
Our technical team, in conjunction with the Trek Leader, constantly monitors the weather conditions and makes informed decisions about proceeding with or aborting the trek.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.
